meta.title: Single Types - Global Configuration
meta.excerpt: Creating single-type collections for global settings, site configuration, and one-off content management.

seo.metaTitle: Single Types in Strapi | CMS Reference
seo.metaDescription: Master single-type collections for global content management.

props.text: Single types are perfect for managing global configuration options and site-wide settings that don't require multiple entries.

props.body:
Site settings
• Contact information
• Theme configuration
• API configuration
• SEO defaults
• Feature flags

props.text: Single types have a complete schema definition similar to collections but with kind set to 'singleType'. Here's a comprehensive example for site settings.

props.title: Complete Site Settings Schema
Single Type Key Difference
props.body: Single types use 'singleType' as the kind value and typically have draftAndPublish set to false since there's only one instance.

props.text: Single types are ideal for site-wide configuration and one-off pages that don't need multiple entries. Here are common use cases.

features.title: Homepage Content
features.description: Hero section, featured content, and homepage-specific blocks

features.title: Navigation
features.description: Main menu items, footer links, and site structure

features.title: Legal Pages
features.description: Privacy policy, terms of service, and cookie policy

features.title: Global Settings
features.description: Site configuration, theme settings, and feature flags

links.title: Homepage Single Type
links.description: Manage hero content, featured articles, testimonials, and CTA sections for the homepage

links.title: Navigation Single Type
links.description: Define main navigation structure, footer menus, and mobile navigation configuration

links.title: Legal Content Single Type
links.description: Store privacy policy, terms of service, and GDPR compliance information

props.title: When to Use Single Types
props.body: Use single types for content that appears once across the site and doesn't need versioning or multiple entries.

props.text: Single types have a simplified API pattern compared to collections. There's no need for ID parameters since there's only one instance.

props.title: Fetching Single Type Data
Updating Single Type Data

props.body:
GET /api/{single-type-name} - Retrieve the single instance
• PUT /api/{single-type-name} - Update the single instance
• No DELETE endpoint (single types always exist)
• Use populate parameter to include relations and components
• No pagination needed since there's only one entry

props.title: API Simplicity
props.body: Single type APIs are simpler than collection APIs since there's no need for ID management, pagination, or filtering.
